Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-21-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Answers To Requests For Production Of Documents Set 3 In Violation Of This Courts Order Of August 1
Off Calendar Jan-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (by Zoom):
Albert Theusen, Esq. 415-787-2648 Theusen Coit Law Group for Plaintiff.
Dolores Donohue, Esq. 925-827-3300 x111 Edrington Schirmer & Murphy LLP for Defendant.

Prior to open court, the clerk informs parties only one proposed order is received. The clerks states the court needs all proposed orders prior to taking the bench. Mr. Theusen states the matter should be off-calendar due to a settlement and is unable to prepare an order.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received on all 4 motions. The clerk informs the court there is only one proposed order received, The Court announces there is no proposed order received for motions 1, 3, and 4. The Court states if proposed orders are not received by the end of this hearing sanctions will be imposed. The Court admonishes parties.

ARGUED. Mr. Theusen apologizes to the Court, violated 8.2(c), email communications to a clerk since December, sanctions as to Plaintiff only, this is a first for counsel, requests an OSC, never been sanctioned, does not contest the court's TR, case has settled, requests motions to be withdrawn. Court comments; papers are 6 inches high, settlement not on docket,did not receive a courtesy copy of notice of settlement; counsel followed efiling procedure, local rules, follow local rules, abuse of discovery process, waste of public resources. The Court denies request for OSC and request to withdraw motions. Ms. Donohue apologizes to the Court, case has settled. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling on all four motions.

2 - Carole Cox's Motion To Compel Further Answers To Requests for Production of Documents Set 3 In Violation [sic] Of This Court's Order Of August 1 is DENIED.

(see word minutes fo full details)

The Court signs four orders.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion For Order Setting "Reasonable Rate" For Copy Of Deposition Transcript
Off Calendar Jul-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs CAROLE COX,and EDWARD COX's Motion For Order Setting "Reasonable Rate" For Copy Of Deposition Transcript.

Plaintiffs Carole Cox and Edward Cox's unopposed Motion For Order Setting "Reasonable Rate" For Copy Of Deposition Transcript is DENIED.

Plaintiffs move under Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.510(c). Section 2025.510 bears the title "Transcription of [deposition] testimony; Costs; Who may obtain copies and when; Retention of stenographic notes; Access to recorded testimony; Stenographic testimony as official record." Subdivision (c) provides as follows: "Notwithstanding subdivision (b) of Section 2025.320, any other party or the deponent, at the expense of that party or deponent, may obtain a copy of the transcript." Section 2025.320(b) is irrelevant to the issues before the court.

Nothing is section 2025.510(c) vests the court with the power to set the "reasonable rate" for copies of deposition transcripts, whether ad hoc or otherwise. Absent authority on point, the court will not and does not weigh in on the "reasonable rate" for copies of deposition transcripts. Surely, any such proceeding would require that the court have personal jurisdiction over the stenographer or service whose rates are being challenged; here, no such personal jurisdiction has been established. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.510(h)(4).) And whatever substantive standard that may apply in such an adjudication, the record here would not support relief. The court is without sufficient evidence to determine under any real standard the stenographer's requested rate was unreasonable or that Plaintiffs' proffered rate is reasonable.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
